title: PPL Solid Phase Extraction for Chemical Exudates from Marine Macrophytes for LC-MS/MS
author: zabinski
materials: Wash equipment with DI water and Alconox
Setting up tubing and rinsing materials.

Materials in addition to the standard procedure: Water bath (for changing milking incubation temperatures) Alternative: to construct your own water bath (per bath, see image 7 for construction) and Materials list Image 7. Example of “Do-It-Yourself” construction of controlled heated water bath with seedling heating pad (made with BioRender). Be sure to insulate the heating pad between the cardboard and tin foil to avoid temperatures fluctuating due to air exchange.
Cartridge Drying and Storage until Elution
Experimental Procedure I: Milking the roots for exudates
Experimental Procedure II: Root exudates and tissue collection
Procedure: Follow instructures as described in Protocols 2-5.

steps.title: Use a 10cm long Rhizon sampler...
steps.instruction: Use a 10cm long Rhizon sampler attached to a syringe to pull porewater from seagrass rhizosphere, pulling from a depth that maximizes root area. When planning this type of experiment, plan to have 2-3 no-plant controls (pot with bare sediment, or outside the meadow if field sampling) to understand metabolite differences across control and experimental groups. The depth you insert the Rhizon sampler may vary based on meadow or mesocosm set up. Tip: you can place a wooden block or PVC tiles within syringes to lock vacuums in place.

steps.title: Draw porewater for 00:15:00 , replacing...
steps.instruction: Draw porewater for 00:15:00 , replacing syringes as needed Image 1. Sampling seagrass rhizosphere porewater from pots in mesocosm using Rhizon sippers.

steps.title: Transfer collected porewater in sterile 50mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer collected porewater in sterile 50mL Falcon tubes and store at -20˚C until SPE.

steps.title: Rinse glassware with DI water
steps.instruction: Rinse glassware with DI water

steps.title: Submergeglassware in 0
steps.instruction: Submergeglassware in 0.1M HCl bath and let soak for 01:00:00

steps.title: Rinse with DI water 2x
steps.instruction: Rinse with DI water 2x

steps.title: Rinse with 100% Methanol
steps.instruction: Rinse with 100% Methanol

steps.title: Cut vacuum tubing to desired length...
steps.instruction: Cut vacuum tubing to desired length with a precise, flat cut.

steps.title: Rinse reservoirs and stopcoks with LCMS-grade...
steps.instruction: Rinse reservoirs and stopcoks with LCMS-grade MeOH followed by LCMS grade H 2 O. Note: You may Parafilm baths after rinsing materials for continued use of baths throughout sample processing.

steps.title: Make 0
steps.instruction: Make 0.01M HCl (acidified H 2 O) by aliquoting 1 L of LCMS-grade H 2 O in acid-washed 1L glass bottle and add 1 mL of 37% LCMS-grade HCl. Note: you may have to make more batches depending on sample size

steps.title: Setting up SPE station
steps.instruction: Setting up SPE station.

steps.title: Connect SPE station to an HDPE...
steps.instruction: Connect SPE station to an HDPE vacuum carboy as a solvent flow-through container, leaving one bulkhead fitting open to prevent a vacuum. Image 2. SPE station set up: Agilent vacuum manifold connected to solvent waste carboy.

steps.title: Cartridge Activation and Pre-Conditioning. Note
steps.instruction: Cartridge Activation and Pre-Conditioning. Note: 1 person can do Extraction Procedure I while another is doing Extraction Procedure II.1-2.

steps.title: Remove lids on vacuum station only for ports in use
steps.instruction: Remove lids on vacuum station only for ports in use.

steps.title: Install PPL cartridges for all samples. Note
steps.instruction: Install PPL cartridges for all samples. Note: Add an extra port set up for an LCMS-grade H 2 O procedural blank each use. Image 3. SPE sample set up: Sample PPL columns with extra PPL column as blank in Agilent SPE vacuum manifold.

steps.title: Wash cartridges as follows to activate...
steps.instruction: Wash cartridges as follows to activate PPL resin, without letting them run dry between washes: a. 3x column volume 100% LCMS grade MeOH b. 3x column volume 0.01M HCl (acidified H 2 O) c. 3x column volume 100% LCMS-grade MeOH d. 3x column volume 0.01M HCl (acidified H 2 O)

steps.title: Aliquot 15 mL of sample into clean, sterile Falcon tube
steps.instruction: Aliquot 15 mL of sample into clean, sterile Falcon tube. Note: place remainder back in fridge, then -20˚C.

steps.title: Acidify samples to 0
steps.instruction: Acidify samples to 0.01M by adding 150 µL of 1M HCl into each sample to acidify (pH = 2.0, typically 0.12% acid for seawater) Note: Can check pH using pH test strips. Note: This will acidify samples to a 0.01M HCl concentration. Note: You will have to adjust calculations depending on total sample volume

steps.title: Pour approximately ~3mL of samples into...
steps.instruction: Pour approximately ~3mL of samples into a reservoir at a time until all sample volume (~15mL total) has passed through the column via gravity (approximately 8mL/min)

steps.title: Flush PPL with 2 column volumes 0
steps.instruction: Flush PPL with 2 column volumes 0.01M HCl (acidified H 2 O) to remove salt from cartridge.

steps.title: Wrap cartridges individually in aluminum foil...
steps.instruction: Wrap cartridges individually in aluminum foil and freeze at -80˚C (or -20˚C if -80˚C is unavailable) or proceed to elution. Note: For field processed samples, without resources to dry, wrap samples in aluminum foil and freeze at -80C until shipment on dry ice for drying and elution.

steps.title: When planning this type of experiment,...
steps.instruction: When planning this type of experiment, plan to have 2-3 no plant controls for each control and treatment group. These controls will be important to understand metabolite differences across all control and experimental groups.

steps.title: Prior to starting the milking procedure, label all necessary material
steps.instruction: Prior to starting the milking procedure, label all necessary material. Each sample being milked should have: a Magenta box or 50mL conical tube per plant 1, 15 mL conical tube for rhizosphere sediment 1, 15 mL conical tube for roots/rhizome tissue 4, 5 mL screw cap tubes for sterilized root exudates 1 Whirlpak for leaves/shoots

steps.title: For the 50 mL conical foam...
steps.instruction: For the 50 mL conical foam floats, label each slot with however many seedling samples are to be milked for exudates Image 2. Example of numbered floats to help keep track of samples in case something tips, or other mishaps occur. Image 3. Magenta box floats that were made from kitchen sponges and pieces attached using a glue gun.

steps.title: Fill spray and squirt bottles with sterile Instant Ocean (IO)
steps.instruction: Fill spray and squirt bottles with sterile Instant Ocean (IO)

steps.title: Pre-wet the magenta box floats so...
steps.instruction: Pre-wet the magenta box floats so that it is easier to fit onto the magenta box

steps.title: Fill each magenta box or conical...
steps.instruction: Fill each magenta box or conical tube by measuring out 20 mL sterile IO. Tip: a bottle top dispenser (such as Varispenser 2 - Eppendorf Catalog No. 4966000053) would be useful at this stage.

steps.title: Assemble the magenta boxes or 50...
steps.instruction: Assemble the magenta boxes or 50 mL conical tubes into their respective float devices and place them in the tank so that the IO in the milking chambers reaches the tank temperature. Measure the temperature of the tank and of the magenta boxes/50 mL conical tubes using thermometers. Image 4. Example of how the milking chambers (magenta boxes and 50 mL conical tubes) look in their respective floats in the tank. Thank you to Sogin Lab member Diane-Marie Branche-Smith for the photo.

steps.title: Gently pull the plant from its...
steps.instruction: Gently pull the plant from its sediment and tap/shake until only the closely adhering sediment is left on the roots/rhizome.

steps.title: Using a squirt bottle containing sterile...
steps.instruction: Using a squirt bottle containing sterile IO, rinse adhering sediment into designated 15 mL conical tubes. a. Once IO is nearing 10-12 mL, stick roots/rhizomes in the tube and gently cap the top of the tube with your thumb (plant leaves are hanging outside of the tube), and shake the tube to remove more of the adhering sediment. The aim is to have ~2-5 “mL” of sediment in the tube.

steps.title: Once the adhering sediment is collected,...
steps.instruction: Once the adhering sediment is collected, use a beaker filled with sterile IO to finish rinsing the plant of its sediment.

steps.title: Place the plant in its designated...
steps.instruction: Place the plant in its designated milking chamber with roots submerged in the sterile IO. Allow the leaves to hang over the magenta boxes or upright in the conical tubes.

steps.title: Allow the roots to be milked for 03:00:00
steps.instruction: Allow the roots to be milked for 03:00:00 . During this time, it is important to spray the leaves with IO to prevent drying out/plant stress. For the first 00:20:00 , keep an eye on how long it takes for the plants to start looking a little dry so that appropriate time can be taken to ensure the leaves are moistened during the milking process.

steps.title: When the milking period is over,...
steps.instruction: When the milking period is over, remove the milking chamber(s) from the tank(s) and remove the plant. These steps also apply to the no plant control chambers.

steps.title: Assemble the 20 mL syringe and filter
steps.instruction: Assemble the 20 mL syringe and filter

steps.title: Remove the plunger and pour the...
steps.instruction: Remove the plunger and pour the 20 mL of root exudates into the syringe and replace the plunger

steps.title: Filter sterilize 5 mL of the...
steps.instruction: Filter sterilize 5 mL of the root exudates into all four 5 mL screw cap tubes

steps.title: Freeze the root exudates, and all...
steps.instruction: Freeze the root exudates, and all other samples, in the liquid nitrogen shipper/dewar

steps.title: Separate the leaves from the roots/rhizome using a scalpel
steps.instruction: Separate the leaves from the roots/rhizome using a scalpel. Place leaves/shoots into their designated Whirlpak. Place roots/rhizosome into their designated 15mL conical tube. Image

steps.title: Example photo of above ground tissue from adult plant
steps.instruction: Example photo of above ground tissue from adult plant. Image

steps.title: Example photo of below ground tissue from adult plant
steps.instruction: Example photo of below ground tissue from adult plant.

steps.title: Experimental Procedure III
steps.instruction: Experimental Procedure III: Modifications for Indoor Tank Set-Up Note: For researchers that are growing Z. marina indoors or within a laboratory setting, these steps offer minor modifications of Protocols 2-5 for capturing milking exudates

steps.title: Make a batch of 1M HCl...
steps.instruction: Make a batch of 1M HCl (to acidify samples) by adding 91.67 mL of LCMS-grade H 2 O then 8.33 mL of LCMS-grade HCl in acid-washed 100mL glass bottle. Note: you may have to make more batches depending on sample size

steps.title: If the PPL columns don't fit...
steps.instruction: If the PPL columns don't fit the vacuum concentrator rotor, remove the rotor and replace with two microtube floats that overlap. Dry the PPL columns in the vacuum concentrator overnight using the following settings: D-AQ with no heat indefinitely (infinity symbol) Note: Ensure that the tips of the columns do not touch the surface of the vacuum concentrator. Note: Alternatively, dry the samples under N 2 gas until dry Image 4: Set up of microtube floats loaded with PPL columns in the vacuum concentrator for drying
